_doc: The Gauge's ONE versioned config: the mapping thresholds. Every threshold the posture mapping and the Sentinel ratchet consume lives here, unit-tested with posture() — never inline in workflow code. Dimension thresholds are min-thresholds: the rule fires at score >= value, and on the 0|1|2 ordinal domain a min of 2 is exactly the '=2' rule. Keys prefixed _doc_ are commentary only (JSON has no comments); consumers ignore them.
_doc_h80_human_hours: Horizon anchor: the human-equivalent hours a current-generation model completes at ~80% success. Re-derived per model generation, not tuned ad hoc. The value holds at 2 for the current builder seats; re-derive when a BUILDER seat changes generation.
_doc_messiness_discount: Real-repo discount on the H80 horizon — benchmark tasks are cleaner than live codebases. Slice budget = h80_human_hours x messiness_discount human-equivalent.
_doc_churn_flips_threshold: Test-failure churn = the same check flipping pass<->fail at least this many times across fix cycles (the Sentinel churn signal).
_doc_rejections_to_feedback_escalation: After this many review rejections on LOGICAL findings, escalate the feedback source (stronger or different-family reviewer) — not the retry count (the Sentinel master signal).
_doc_rejections_to_split: After this many logical-finding rejections, stop retrying: split-and-rebuild the slice.
_doc_deescalation_clean_window: Consecutive clean units (zero rejections AND a first-pass green gate) required before posture returns to the Gauge baseline at a unit boundary (hysteresis — K8s/Redwood pattern).
_doc_research_topics_base: research row: the cap on research topics is research_topics_base + D3 + D5. The research stage applies min(OQ-count, cap), and 0 when no high-priority before-build OQs exist — OQ data is runtime input, not a profile dimension.
_doc_planning_dual_d4_min: planning row, dual trigger 1: dual-plan + chairman when D4 (fork risk) >= this. Canonical: D4=2.
_doc_planning_dual_d3_min: planning row, dual trigger 2, first conjunct: D3 (novelty) >= this AND D1 >= planning_dual_d1_min. Canonical: D3>=1 AND D1>=1.
_doc_planning_dual_d1_min: planning row, dual trigger 2, second conjunct: D1 (scope size) >= this.
_doc_planning_redteam_d4_min: planning row, red-team tier: D4 >= this OR D8 >= planning_redteam_d8_min. Checked only after dual declined, so with the default dual threshold this >= is exactly the D4=1 rule.
_doc_planning_redteam_d8_min: planning row, red-team tier's other disjunct: D8 (failure penalty) >= this. Canonical: D8>=1.
_doc_plan_validation_rounds_base: plan-validation row: rounds = base + 1 when D2 >= plan_validation_d2_min + 1 when D8 >= plan_validation_d8_min.
_doc_plan_validation_d2_min: plan-validation row: +1 round when D2 (ambiguity) >= this. Canonical: D2>=1.
_doc_plan_validation_d8_min: plan-validation row: +1 round when D8 >= this. Canonical: D8=2 (maximum failure penalty).
_doc_slice_budget_d7_min: slice-budget rule: apply d7_slice_budget_factor when D7 (verifiability) >= this. Canonical: D7>=1.
_doc_d7_slice_budget_factor: slice-budget rule: the budget is multiplied by this (halved) when verifiability is weak (D7 >= slice_budget_d7_min) — harder-to-verify work ships in smaller slices.
_doc_review_high_d8_min: slice-review row: ui codex review effort flips medium->high when D8 >= this. Canonical: D8>=1. The fix-cycle>0 half of the trigger is runtime — posture names it via review.escalate_on.
_doc_min_slices_for_tribunal: milestone-gate row: the dual-analyst tribunal runs only when a milestone has at least this many slices; a single-slice milestone's gate is slice review + goal-backward (tribunal redundancy skip, a proven-safe class).
_doc_trivial_tier_dim_max: scope-tier predicate: the ceiling for every dimension NOT in trivial_tier_soft_dims. Non-compensatory — no sums; a lone D8=1 or D4=1 must never read trivial (D4/D8 are structurally outside the soft set). A profile with D6=1 alone (the rubric's own 'single store, simple schema' example) reads trivial; D1=1,D2=1,D6=1,D7=1 reads standard; a high-dims/effort-0 trap reads standard.
_doc_trivial_tier_soft_dims: The NAMED benign dimensions allowed up to trivial_tier_soft_dim_max while the tier still reads trivial. One entry per proven case: D6 (data & state) — the canonical trivial fixture (todo CLI, one flat JSON file) honestly scores D6=1 because ANY persistent store does; demanding D6=0 made the trivial tier empty in practice. Extend only with evidence.
_doc_trivial_tier_soft_dim_max: The ceiling for the soft dimensions above. 1 = the rubric's 'single store, simple schema'; 2 (migrations/concurrency/multi-tenant) is never trivial.
_doc_tribunal_threshold_trivial_bump: tribunal-vs-posture lever: at scope_tier 'trivial' the effective tribunal threshold is min_slices_for_tribunal + this bump (2->3 at defaults); at 'standard', unchanged. At trivial scope the tribunal is marginal — one MEDIUM beyond the slice reviews; at standard scope it is decisive. Goal-backward NEVER skips at any tier.
_doc_browser_tier2_d7_min: browser row: Tier-2 traversal per ui milestone when D7 >= this OR D8 >= browser_tier2_d8_min. Canonical: D7>=1.
_doc_browser_tier2_d8_min: browser row's other disjunct: D8 >= this. Canonical: D8>=1.
_doc_validate_adversarial_d8_min: validate row: the adversarial probe pass switches on when D8 >= this. Canonical: D8=2.
_doc_validate_second_family_d8_min: validate row: a second validator family switches on when D8 >= this. Canonical: D8=2 — its own knob so the two extras stay independently overridable.
_doc_effort_bias_dims: model/effort row: the effort dial is the MAX of these dimensions' scores (non-compensatory, per call class). Canonical: max(D3, D4, D8).
